results.title: War and Peace | Author, How Many Pages, Book, Summary, Characters, & Facts | Britannica
results.snippet: Leo Tolstoy Russian writer Leo Tolstoy is the author of the classic novel *War and Peace* (1865–69). **War and Peace**, historical novel by Russian author Leo Tolstoy, originally published as *Voyna i mir* in 1865–69. He visited battlefields, read history books on the Napoleonic Wars, and drew on real historical events to create a novel of living history. As Tolstoy wrote and revised, however, the novel evolved into the *War and Peace* known today—a novel that takes place more than a decade before the Decembrist movement. Many of the family names used in *War and Peace* are slight alterations of real names Tolstoy had encountered in his life—a deliberate strategy intended to make the novel feel familiar to the Russians who read it. Tolstoy wrote *War and Peace* over the course of six years. For a discussion of *War and Peace* in the context of Tolstoy’s life and work, *see* Leo Tolstoy: The period of the great novels (1863–77).
